The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF ANDY SIU's Motion For Fees And Costs After Prevailing On Appeal is GRANTED. Mr. Siu is entitled to $19,710.00 in fees and $776.59 in costs against defendants Oscar Martinez and M&M Construction. No opposition filed and good cause shown. MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-19-2015 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternqative Summary Adjudication Of Claims By Plaintiff Andy Siu Against Oscar Martinez
Off Calendar Jan-26-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL, Tel No. (415) 788-7600 appearing for Plaintiff Andy Siu;
Atty. GARY B. WESLEY, Tel No. (408) 882-5070 appearing for Defendant Oscar Martinez.

RULING - COUNSEL STIPULATE THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL MAY E-FILE HIS CORRECTED DECLARATION THAT CONTAINS PAGES MISSING FROM THE DECLARATION PREVIOUSLY E-FILED, AND THAT HIS DECLARATION WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED LATE. Matter argued by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ANDY SIU' Motion for Summary Judgment Of Claims By Plaintiff Andy Siu Against Oscar Martinez is granted. Defendant Martinez admits that he was a general partner of M&M Construction and a judgment was entered against that entity. Defendant is jointly and severally liable under Cal. Corp. Code sec. 16306. The court rejects Defendant's argument that the renewed judgment was void. First, it is unclear whether the judgment that was renewed actually has the correct amount of money. The original judgment was for $221,088.46 and the judgment that was renewed was for $151,742.72 plus interest after judgment (total $303,390.55). Even if the renewed amount was improper, then M&M should have objected and sought to vacate or modify the renewal. Second, the Court of Appeal affirmed the judgment and an error regarding interest does not render the entire judgment void. See Dakota Payphone, LLC v. Alcaraz (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 493, 508. Plaintiff's continued prosecution of this action against M&M after defendant filed for bankruptcy did not violate the bankruptcy stay. See Danko v. O'Reilly (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 732, 745. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)
